source_item.title: Spacetech unicorn Skyroot launches Vikram-1, marks India's first private orbital rocket mission

slide1_hook.label: India's First Private Rocket Just Reached Orbit
slide1_hook.subhead: A startup just did what only ISRO could before.

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: For Decades, Only ISRO Could Launch Rockets
slide2_backstory.body: India only let ISRO launch rockets until 2020, when the sector opened up to private firms. Ex-ISRO engineers Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka founded Skyroot in 2018 and reached space in 2022 on a test flight.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: Vikram-1 Lifts Off, All Four Stages Succeed
slide3_news.body: Skyroot's Vikram-1 rocket lifted off on July 18, 2026, from Sriharikota and completed all four stages in 17 minutes. It put satellites into orbit 450 km up, making Skyroot the first Indian private company to reach orbit.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: India's Commercial Space Race Just Sped Up
bullets: Skyroot plans 4 to 6 launches this year
India's space economy could hit $44 billion by 2033
Govt subsidy of 30% makes Skyroot cheaper than rivals

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: Skyroot just did what only ISRO could do before. Should ISRO feel threatened, or proud?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for daily business stories that matter to India 🚀
